response.description: A dropdown menu for displaying a list of options or actions that are revealed upon user interaction.
response.semanticDescription: The Dropdown-menu component provides an accessible and customizable way to present a list of interactive items, such as actions or options, in a compact overlay. It typically appears when a trigger element (e.g., a button) is clicked or hovered. Key features include keyboard navigation, customizable content, and flexible positioning. Use cases include user profile menus, action lists for data tables, filtering options, and contextual navigation.

guidance.accessibility: Ensure proper keyboard navigation (Tab to open/close, Arrow keys to navigate items, Enter/Space to select). Implement appropriate ARIA attributes like `aria-haspopup`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and `role='menu'` on the menu and `role='menuitem'` on items.
guidance.whenToUse: Use a Dropdown-menu to group a list of related actions or options, saving space and presenting them contextually. Ideal for navigation, filtering, or action lists that don't need to be constantly visible.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Avoid using for only one or two options; use a simple Button or Toggle instead. Do not use for critical, immediate actions that require high visibility, or when all options must be visible simultaneously.
